name: pyairtable (Python Client for Airtable API)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Gui Talarico; Airtable API costs apply

performance.notes: REST API; 5 req/s rate limit; built-in retry with backoff

agent_readiness.notes: Python client for Airtable API. from pyairtable import Api. api = Api('YOUR_TOKEN'). table = api.table('appXXX', 'tblXXX'). records = table.all(). table.create({'Name': 'Alice', 'Email': 'alice@example.com'}). table.update('recXXX', {'Name': 'Bob'}). table.delete('recXXX'). Batch: table.batch_create(list_of_dicts). Formula filter: table.all(formula=AND('Name'='Alice')). View filter. sort. Fields filter. Auto-pagination. Retry on 429. Webhook management. ORM-like: table.to_pandas(). Field types: Attachment, Formula, Lookup.

gotchas: 5 requests/second rate limit — batch operations required for large datasets
Airtable record IDs (recXXX) are not stable across table exports
Free tier limited to 1000 records per base
best_when: Reading/writing Airtable databases from Python scripts
Syncing data between Airtable and other systems
Using Airtable as a lightweight CMS or operational database
avoid_when: High-volume data operations (Airtable is not a production database)
When Airtable's 1000-row free tier limit is a constraint
summary: pyairtable is a Python client for the Airtable API. Api('TOKEN').table('appXXX', 'tblXXX'). table.all(), .create(), .update(), .delete(). Batch operations, formula filters, view filters, sort. Auto-pagination. Retry on rate limits (5 req/s). Webhook management. to_pandas() for DataFrame conversion. Best for automating Airtable-based workflows. MIT license. For high-volume data use a proper database.
